question: IS LATE THUMB A PROBLEM ON REV RATE?
content:
yes. late thumb makes the ball slide but not rev
Why late thumb destroys revs
Because when the thumb exits late:
The ball stays on the entire hand too long, so you can’t get the ball fully onto the fingers.
The ball slides forward, instead of sitting deep on the fingers to lift.
The fingers lose leverage → less lift, less rotation, fewer revs.
When the thumb exits early and clean, the fingers take over. That alone can raise your revs dramatically without changing anything else in your game.
